semiotic.positioning: The bitten apple is the most valuable brand mark in the world. Apple's visual language — white space, San Francisco typeface, product-as-object photography — is a self-consistent semiotic system requiring no explanatory text.

narrative.positioning: The garage-to-empire founding story, Jobs's return from exile, and the Think Different campaign constitute one of the most complete brand mythologies in corporate history. The 1984 Super Bowl ad remains the canonical moment of brand narrative as cultural event.

ideological.positioning: Privacy as a human right (post-2014) and creativity as the defining human attribute are Apple's two primary ideological claims. Both are genuine differentiators in the competitive landscape, though tension exists between privacy rhetoric and ad-targeting business interests.
key_signals: Privacy is a human right positioning

experiential.positioning: Apple Retail stores are the highest-revenue-per-square-foot retail locations in the world. The ecosystem lock-in (iPhone + Mac + iPad + Watch + AirPods) creates compounding experiential integration that makes switching costly at the habit level, not just the financial level.

social.positioning: iPhone ownership functions as a global status signal, amplified by the green vs. blue bubble dynamic in iMessage. The brand has evolved from counterculture identity to mainstream aspirational status, particularly among younger demographics.
key_signals: iPhone camera quality as social currency
Apple Watch as health status signal

economic.positioning: Apple operates at the premium end of every category it enters and has never competed on price. The Services revenue layer (App Store, iCloud, Apple TV+) adds margin on top of already high-margin hardware, compounding economic exclusivity.

cultural.positioning: Silicon Valley's design philosophy is, in large part, Apple's design philosophy exported globally. The brand is culturally synonymous with the idea that technology should be beautiful, and with the creative professional as archetype.
key_signals: Dieter Rams influence on Jony Ive

temporal.positioning: Founded 1976, Apple has 50 years of history but deliberately presents as forward-looking rather than heritage-oriented. The Jobs mythology is the primary temporal anchor; post-Jobs Apple has maintained continuity while consciously avoiding nostalgia as a brand tool.
